MELItem.Title: Trailing Edge Variable Camber (Cruise Flaps) Function

ListItem.Para: Flap secondary mode is verified to operate normally.
Spoiler actuator system operates normally.
Appropriate weight and center-of-gravity limits are observed.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

Note.Para: Use Boeing Onboard Performance Tool (OPT) to determine take-off weight and/or landing weight as it affects aircraft performance.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify operation of flap secondary mode (AMM DDG 27-51-02).

ListItem.Para: Do the ground test of the flap secondary mode.
Confirm flaps are UP.
For all three hydraulic systems available, turn hydraulics on.
For only one or two of the hydraulic systems available at once:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever UP.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are extended.
Conduct the ground system test Flaps (Electric).

Note.Para: If hydraulic systems are pressurized, spoilers, ailerons, and flaperons will move during test.

ListItem.Para: After completion of the test, confirm the FLAPS SECONDARY FAIL status message is not displayed.
Retract spoilers if extended:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever DN.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are retracted.
The maximum takeoff weight is limited to:

Effectivities.Effectivity: For 787-9 (VN-A861, VN-A862, VN-A863, VN-A864, VN-A865, VN-A866, VN-A867, VN-A868, VN-A869, VN-A870, V-A871): Take-off weight limit is 523,300 lb (237,364 kg)
For 787- 10 (VN-A872, VN-A873, VN-A874, VN-A877, VN-A878): Take-off weight limit is 544,800 lb lb (247,117 kg)

ListItem.Para: For cruise flaps inoperative in combination with flap position/skew sensors inoperative (MEL item 27-58-01), increase flight planning fuel by either incrementing the current the airplane drag correction factor by 1.0% or increase the flight planning fuel by 1.2%.
Flap secondary mode is inoperative.
Spoiler actuator system operates normally.
Appropriate weight and center-of-gravity limits are observed.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

Note.Para: Use Boeing Onboard Performance Tool (OPT) to determine take-off weight and/or landing weight as it affects aircraft performance.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Revised (O) to cover all the performance penalties included in -02A

ListItem.Para: Use MEL 27-51-02-01A (O) procedure.
Flap secondary mode is verified to operate normally.
Spoiler actuator system operates normally.
Appropriate weight and center-of-gravity limits are observed.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

Note.Para: Use Boeing Onboard Performance Tool (OPT) to determine take-off weight and/or landing weight as it affects aircraft performance.

The CRUISE FLAPS DISAGREE status message indicates a failure of the cruise flaps system in a minor inboard / outboard split configuration, or out of the faired position. These conditions are within the normal range of cruise flaps operation and may not be visually apparent.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify operation of flap secondary mode (AMM DDG 27-51-02).

ListItem.Para: Do the ground test of the flap secondary mode.
Confirm flaps are UP.
For all three hydraulic systems available, turn hydraulics on.
For only one or two of the hydraulic systems available at once:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever UP.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are extended.
Conduct the ground system test Flaps (Electric).

Note.Para: If hydraulic systems are pressurized, spoilers, ailerons, and flaperons will move during test.

ListItem.Para: After completion of the test, confirm the FLAPS SECONDARY FAIL status message is not displayed.
Retract spoilers if extended:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever DN.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are retracted.
Use MEL 27-51-02-01A (O) procedure weight and center-of-gravity limits.
For cruise flaps inoperative only, increase flight planning fuel by either incrementing the current airplane drag correction factor by 3.5% or increase the flight planning fuel by 4.0%.

Flaps inoperative in combination with flap position/skew sensors inoperative (MEL item 27-58-01), increase flight planning fuel by either incrementing the current the airplane drag correction factor by 4.1% or increase the flight planning fuel by 4.7%.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Revised step so that it can be used by -02A and -02B dispatch option.

ListItem.Para: Reduce performance limited weight by:

entry.Para: 12,600 lb (5,715 kg) (when combined with MEL item 27-58-01)
12,200 lb (5,534 kg) (when combined with MEL item 27-58-01)

Note.Para: The enroute climb weight penalties are based on single engine operating speeds that approximate maximum lift-to-drag ratio speed. To account for the difference in level-off altitude when operating at other speeds, multiply the enroute climb weight penalty listed above by the appropriate factor listed on page 3.00-01-00.2 in the ENROUTE DIVERSION SPEED EFFECTS paragraph.

ListItem.Para: Flap secondary mode is inoperative.
Spoiler actuator system operates normally.
Appropriate weight and center-of-gravity limits are observed.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

Note.Para: Use Boeing Onboard Performance Tool (OPT) to determine take-off weight and/or landing weight as it affects aircraft performance.

MaintenanceNote.Para: The CRUISE FLAPS DISAGREE status message indicates a failure of the cruise flaps system in a minor inboard / outboard split configuration, or out of the faired position. These conditions are within the normal range of cruise flaps operation and may not be visually apparent.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Revised (O) to cover all the performance penalties included in -02A

ListItem.Para: Use MEL 27-51-02-02A (O) procedure.
